tasks.title: Define canonical dataset-boundary contract and rerun validation categories

tasks.title: Add query analytics, feedback capture, and learned relevance tuning for scheduler retrieval
tasks.description: Instrument scheduler narrative search with durable query analytics, result or click feedback, and learned relevance-tuning signals so boolean, full-text, and semantic ranking quality can be evaluated and improved from operator behavior instead of only static heuristics.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit 335d184 with scheduler query analytics persistence, feedback capture, heuristic learned_score and tuning_signals surfaced through the retrieval payload, and control-room Relevant or Not relevant actions plus analytics panels wired into the scheduler search experience.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after targeted API pytest coverage, compileall verification, web typecheck/build, docs status updates, commit, and push all landed; the latest main-session reply now leaves operator-facing analytics dashboard and moderation workflow productization plus heavier offline reranker infrastructure as the follow-ups.

tasks.title: Add scheduler query analytics dashboard and feedback moderation workflow
tasks.description: Productize the new scheduler retrieval analytics and feedback loop with a dedicated query analytics dashboard, operator-facing feedback review or moderation flows, and reusable quality views so search quality can be monitored and corrected without inspecting raw per-query payloads.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit 44b5907 with dedicated scheduler query analytics dashboard routes and control-room surfaces, moderation-gated feedback status plus moderator metadata persistence, moderation queue actions, and learned tuning restricted to approved feedback.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after backend and control-room workflow wiring, targeted pytest coverage, compileall verification, web typecheck/build, docs alignment, commit, and push all landed; the latest main-session reply now leaves longer-horizon search-quality dashboarding or moderation governance plus heavier offline reranker infrastructure as the explicit follow-ups.

tasks.title: Add long-horizon scheduler search quality dashboard and moderation governance layer
tasks.description: Extend the new query analytics dashboard and moderation queue into a longer-horizon quality operations surface with trend views, governance states, and policy-oriented moderation workflows so scheduler search quality can be reviewed and governed over time instead of only through immediate query rollups.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit bf5e7c7 with long-horizon scheduler search quality dashboard filters, governance view selectors, daily trend and actor or moderator rollups, stale or high-score backlog views, and batch moderation actions wired across backend, API, control-room, tests, and docs.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after targeted scheduler provenance pytest coverage, compileall verification, web typecheck and build, docs updates, commit, and push all landed; the latest main-session reply now leaves a dedicated moderation policy catalog or staged approval queue as the immediate governance continuation ahead of heavier offline reranker infrastructure.

tasks.title: Add moderation policy catalog and staged approval queue for scheduler governance
tasks.description: Extend the shipped long-horizon scheduler quality dashboard and batch moderation layer with reusable moderation policy catalog records plus a staged approval queue so operators can govern feedback actions under durable review rules instead of only direct queue mutations.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit aa59f0d with moderation policy catalog records and staged approval queue support wired through scheduler governance backend surfaces, external search persistence, control-room moderation workflows, and matching docs updates.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after targeted pytest coverage, compileall verification, web typecheck or build, commit, and push all landed; the latest main-session reply now leaves moderation-catalog lifecycle or audit or bulk-governance depth plus heavier offline reranker infrastructure as the remaining direct follow-ups.

tasks.title: Add lifecycle, audit, and bulk governance workflows for moderation policy catalogs
tasks.description: Deepen the newly shipped scheduler moderation policy catalog layer by adding catalog edit or delete or versioning flows, team audit visibility, and bulk governance actions so reusable moderation rules can be maintained safely after create, stage, approve, and apply are already in place.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit ba0286a with moderation policy catalogs promoted into lifecycle objects carrying revision and team-audit persistence, update or delete or revisions or restore or audits or bulk-governance API routes, external scheduler search persistence, and expanded control-room edit or delete or restore or team-audit or multi-select governance workflows.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed after targeted pytest coverage, compileall verification, web typecheck/build, docs current-state alignment, commit, and push all landed; the latest main-session reply now leaves catalog-specific staged approval queues and reusable governance policy layering as the immediate continuation ahead of heavier retrieval-quality infrastructure.

tasks.title: Add staged approval queue and reusable governance policy layer for moderation policy catalogs
tasks.description: Extend the newly lifecycle-aware moderation policy catalog object with catalog-specific staged approval queue workflows and a reusable governance policy layer so catalog edits, restores, and bulk-governance changes can be reviewed and routed under durable moderation rules instead of only direct lifecycle mutations.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply explicitly says moderation policy catalogs now cover lifecycle, audit, and bulk governance, but catalog-specific staged approval queues and a reusable governance policy layer are still missing, making this the clearest executable continuation.
tasks.priority_reason: Promoted to ready because builtin next-task should keep advancing the operator-governance sequence named in the latest main-session reply before shifting to offline reranker and learned relevance infrastructure.

tasks.title: Add offline evaluation and learned reranker infrastructure for scheduler retrieval
tasks.description: Extend the new feedback-driven heuristic tuning into heavier relevance infrastructure by adding offline evaluation datasets, replayable ranking experiments, and a learned reranker path so scheduler search quality can improve beyond online heuristics and manually reviewed feedback loops.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply still calls out offline reranker, learned model training, and heavier relevance infrastructure as missing after ba0286a shipped lifecycle, audit, and bulk governance for moderation policy catalogs, so this should build on the now-governed feedback baseline rather than bypass the remaining catalog approval-layer gap.
tasks.priority_reason: This remains the next retrieval-quality investment, but it stays proposed behind the newly explicit moderation catalog staged-approval and reusable-governance continuation so builtin next-task advances through one direct operator-governance hardening step first.

tasks.title: Add operator-facing retention and prune controls for shared provenance exports
tasks.description: Expose retention policy, prune actions, and related operator-visible safeguards for the new shared provider provenance export registry so server-side export jobs, artifacts, and audit history can be managed without shell-only cleanup workflows.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply explicitly calls out the absence of operator-facing prune or retention control UI after the shared registry and audit-trail slice shipped.
tasks.priority_reason: This is executable follow-up work, but it remains proposed behind the analytics slice so the queue advances through one immediate consumer of the new registry before shifting into operator policy and lifecycle controls.

tasks.title: Let browser-local provenance history entries promote into the shared registry
tasks.description: Add a dedicated action that promotes existing browser-local provider provenance history entries into the shared export registry so older local handoffs can be preserved server-side without rerunning the original copy flow.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply explicitly notes that browser-local history entries still lack a separate action to be promoted into the new shared registry after the first shared export flow shipped.
tasks.priority_reason: This is executable follow-up work, but it stays proposed behind the analytics queue head so the next task keeps building broad shared-registry visibility before adding migration ergonomics for legacy local entries.

tasks.title: Expand risk-aware primary-focus scoring beyond guarded-live market-data alerts
tasks.description: Generalize the new explicit multi-symbol primary_focus contract so non-market-data multi-symbol alerts use risk-aware scoring rather than falling back to runtime or live symbol order, keeping operator focus selection consistent across alert classes.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply says explicit primary_focus is now emitted broadly, but risk-aware scoring still only applies to guarded-live market-data alerts while other multi-symbol alerts fall back to runtime or live symbol order.
tasks.priority_reason: This is executable follow-up work, but it stays proposed behind external payload propagation so the queue advances through one contract-hardening step at a time.

tasks.title: Add operator action workflow for multi-symbol triage incidents
tasks.description: Connect the control-room triage panel to operator actions for multi-symbol alerts and incidents so ack, escalation, and remediation workflows can launch from the chosen primary focus while preserving visibility into the remaining candidate symbols.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply explicitly calls out missing operator action workflow after the multi-symbol primary-focus policy shipped, so this proposal now targets action handling on top of the chosen focus and candidate-order context.
tasks.priority_reason: This is executable follow-up work, but it remains proposed behind backend payload metadata and filtering/export so the queue advances through the remaining hardening sequence without splitting focus.

tasks.title: Interpret rerun lineage categories in operator-facing UI
tasks.description: Update operator-facing frontend views to actively interpret rerun lineage categories and dataset-boundary claims instead of showing opaque matched or drifted states.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completed on main in commit 58308b8 via control-room summary cards and validation summary rendering in App.tsx, controlRoomDefinitions.ts, and styles.css.
tasks.priority_reason: Closed because the UI now interprets the rerun lineage categories directly for operators instead of exposing only raw taxonomy state.

tasks.title: Design durable strategy registry schema and repository contract
tasks.description: Move custom strategy registration from process-local state into a durable registry contract with queryable records, repository semantics, and restart-safe identity.
tasks.direction_relation_note: This is the first Experiment OS slice in the fixed Batch 1 order from the execution board and latest main-session reply.
tasks.priority_reason: It is the registry foundation for lifecycle durability, promotion state, and normalized experiment queries.

tasks.title: Wire durable lifecycle and promotion state into strategy records and filters
tasks.description: Extend the strategy registry plan with durable lifecycle and promotion records that remain queryable across restart and flow into common experiment filters.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Matches the lifecycle and promotion durability slice listed after the durable registry in the Batch 1 ordering.
tasks.priority_reason: Should not be `ready` until the durable registry contract exists, because lifecycle semantics depend on persistent strategy identity.

tasks.title: Define normalized experiment summary, artifact registry, and export registry model
tasks.description: Plan the normalized experiment summary model plus artifact and export registry paths so common Experiment OS queries stop depending on payload-only scans.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Corresponds to the experiment summary, artifact, and export registry slice called out in the execution board and direction detail doc.
tasks.priority_reason: This remains immediate Batch 1 work, but it should follow the registry foundation so strategy identity and lifecycle can anchor common query paths.

tasks.title: Make benchmark-pack and promotion-review artifacts first-class
tasks.description: Add the follow-up slice that promotes benchmark-pack and promotion-review artifacts from loose payloads into first-class Experiment OS records.
tasks.direction_relation_note: Completes the final unfinished Experiment OS item listed under Batch 1 exit work.
tasks.priority_reason: This should remain downstream because benchmark-pack and promotion-review artifacts depend on the durable lifecycle and normalized artifact model.
